Logical scrutiny is plausible but the delegation does not name it.","R1.3":"The causal-attribution claim (SVDD family dominance ⟹ clean attribution of AMT quality lift) and the 'uniformly outperform' generalization cite no in-paper experiments or tables; evidence-claim alignment review is plausible but unspecified.","R1.6":"The second paragraph consists largely of comma-spliced run-on sentences; sentence-level copyediting is a natural reading of 'take a look' but not the only one."},"clarification_needed":true,"clarification_question":"Should I focus on the logical and methodological soundness of the claims (e.g., whether the 'uniformly outperform' assertion and the causal attribution of AMT quality lift to the gating step are justified), or on sentence-level writing quality and readability?"}

rationale.R1.2: The artifact makes several sweeping methodological assertions—deep detectors 'uniformly outperform' classical baselines 'across the full range of settings,' and any downstream quality lift can be 'attributed entirely' to the gate—each of which has a non-trivial logical gap. Logical scrutiny is plausible but the delegation does not name it.
rationale.R1.3: The causal-attribution claim (SVDD family dominance ⟹ clean attribution of AMT quality lift) and the 'uniformly outperform' generalization cite no in-paper experiments or tables; evidence-claim alignment review is plausible but unspecified.
rationale.R1.6: The second paragraph consists largely of comma-spliced run-on sentences; sentence-level copyediting is a natural reading of 'take a look' but not the only one.

parsed.clarification_question: Should I focus on the logical and methodological soundness of the claims (e.g., whether the 'uniformly outperform' assertion and the causal attribution of AMT quality lift to the gating step are justified), or on sentence-level writing quality and readability?
